revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hikoyai]][[ja:日子八井命]][[simple:Hikoyai]][[zh:彥八井耳命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|father={{ill|Jimmu|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}|mother={{ill|Himetataraisuzuhime|qid=Q5353793|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|1=Emperor Suizei}}; {{ill|Kamuyaimimi-no-mikoto|qid=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}; {{ill|Kishimi Mino Mikoto|qid=Q13257020|1=Kisumimi}}; {{ill|Tagishimimi|qid=Q11075750|1=Tagishimimi}}}}
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese Prince}}
{{refimprove|date=January 2022}}
{{Infobox noble|reign-type=[[神武天皇]]の{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|birth_date=unknown|death_date=April 4th|death_place={{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}|father={{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}|mother=[[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]}} {{Nihongo|{{ill|Hikoyai|en|Hikoyai|qid=Q11086120|lt=Hikoyai-no-Mikoto|1=Hikoyai}}|日子八井命}}<ref name="ja吉川弘文館-2010a">{{Cite book |author= |title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版 |publisher=[[:ja:吉川弘文館]] |year=2010 |isbn=9784642014588 |editor= |trans-title=Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clan Names Popular Edition |chapter=日子八井命 |ref={{Harvid|日子八井命（古代氏族）|2010th year }}}}</ref><ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2022a">{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=デジタル版 |title=日子八井命とは |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%97%A5%E5%AD%90%E5%85%AB%E4%BA%95%E5%91%BD-1103305 |access-date=2022-06-01 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref> was a member of a Japanese imperial clan from the [[Kojiki]] and other sources.<ref name="https-2608a" /><ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2022a" /> He is one of the three princes born to [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] on the banks of the {{ill|Sakai River (Gifu)|en|Sakai River (Gifu)|qid=Q4330698|lt=Sakai River|1=Sakai River (Gifu)}}, and the eldest of the three.<ref name="日本人名大辞典+plus-2022a" /><ref>{{Cite journal |last=ANDASSOVA |first=Maral |date=2019 |title=Emperor Jinmu in the Kojiki |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26652947 |journal=Japan Review |issue=32 |pages=5–16 |issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Visit Kusakabeyoshimi Shrine on your trip to Takamori-machi or Japan |url=https://trips.klarna.com:443/en/adp/japan/takamori-machi/kusakabeyoshimi-shrine-a4534630399/ |access-date=2023-03-04 |website=trips.klarna.com |language=en}}</ref> The name does not appear in the "Nihon Shoki" and there are no other names that are believed to be the same person. Hikoyai-no-Mikoto is the first son of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} and Empress [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]]. His brothers are [[Kamuyaimimi]] and {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|lt=Emperor Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}}. After the death of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, Empress [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] marries her stepson {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|Tagishimimi|qid=Q11075750|lt=Tagishimimi|1=Tagishimimi}}, who would be considered a step-brother to Hikoyai-no-Mikoto and his brothers. {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|Tagishimimi|qid=Q11075750|lt=Tagishimimi|1=Tagishimimi}} then attempts to assassinate the brothers, but instead is killed by {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|lt=Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}} who becomes the second emperor. Hikoyai-no-Mikoto does not have any significant role in the story and does not play an active role in the narrative. This is not uncommon in Japanese myths, where there are often brothers who do not play an active role in the story.<ref name="https-2608a">https://nihonsinwa.com/page/2608.html</ref>
== Genealogy ==
{{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
According to [[Kojiki]], he was the son of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, the first emperor of Japan, and [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]] daughter of [[Ōmononushi|Omononushi no Mikoto]].<ref name="ja吉川弘文館-2010a" />
On the other hand, there is no mention of his name in the [[Nihon Shoki]], and in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Seijiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the Ukyo Emperor's separate Ibarada-ren article and the "Aso Family Brief Genealogical Record", there is a different theory that claims that he is the son of Kamiyai-mimi-no-mikoto.<ref name="ja吉川弘文館-2010a" /> In fact, the name of Hikoyamimikoto does not appear in the "Aso Family Tree" or the "Monzan Family Tree" in the 16th volume of the "Shoshu Keifu".<ref>Hoga Hisao, "Chapter 2: Imperial Clans, Section 1: The Many Clans", Ancient Clans Genealogical Collection, Vol. 1, Ancient Clans Study Group, 1986, p. 210.</ref>
The "{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}" commissioned by {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|qid=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}} and the "{{Nihongo|Aso family tree|阿蘇家略系譜|Aso-ka ryaku keifu}}" list a different theory that {{ill|Hikoyai|en|Hikoyai|qid=Q11086120|lt=Hikoyai|1=Hikoyai}} was the son of [[Kamuyaimimi]].<ref>「日子八井命」『日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版』[[吉川弘文館]]、2010th year 。</ref>。
== Descendant clans == In the [[Kojiki]], Hikoyai is listed as the ancestor of Ibarada-ren, Teshima-ren, and others.<ref name="ja吉川弘文館-2010a" />
In {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsengumi Seijiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, the following clans are listed as descendants.<ref name="ja吉川弘文館-2010a" />
* Ibarata Ren by Emperor Ukyo -The same ancestor of Tachoomi . After Kamuyaimimi no Hikoyai no Mikoto. * Ibarata Ren by Emperor Yamashiro-Ibarata Sukune's ancestor. After Hikoyai no Mikoto. * Toyoshima Ren by Emperor Settsu --- Tachoomi's ancestor. After Hikoyai no Mikoto. * Settsu Emperor Matsutsu Neck-Toyoshima Ren's ancestor. * Sukune Ibarata by Emperor Kawachi --- Tachoomi's ancestor. After Hikoyai no Mikoto. In the same article, the name of Nomi no Sukune is given to the child. * Kawachi Emperor Betsu Shimoieren-After Hikoyai-no-Mikoto. * Kawachi Emperor Betsu Eshu-Hikoyai-no-Mikoto After the 7th grandson, Kurume Tsuhiko-mei. * Owari-be by Emperor Kawachi-after Hikoyai-no-Mikoto.
== Notes ==
* The son of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} . He is the elder brother of the second {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|lt=Emperor Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}}.<ref name="genbunet-2022a">{{Cite web |title=日子八井命：玄松子の祭神記 |url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hikoyai.htm |access-date=2022-06-29 |website=genbu.net |language=ja}}</ref> * According to "[[Kojiki]]", Emperor Jimmu was enthroned and married to [[Himetataraisuzu-hime|Himetataraisuzu]] , and the three pillars of {{ill|Hikoyai|en|Hikoyai|qid=Q11086120|lt=Hikoyai|1=Hikoyai}}, [[Kamuyaimimi]], and {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|lt=Emperor Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}}.<ref name="genbunet-2022a" /> * It is said that Hikoyaimimi did not appear in "Nihon Shoki", and only Kamuyaimimi and Kamuyaimimikawa were born between Himetataraisuzuhime and Himetataraisuzuhime. In "Kujiki Kujiki", Hikoyai-no-Mikoto is the son of his younger brother, Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto.<ref name="genbunet-2022a" /> * Also, in "Kojiki", the brothers of Kamuyaimimi and Kamuyaimimi killed their half-brother, Shimi Mimei, and at that time, Kamuyaimimi was shy, so his younger brother, Kamuyaimimi. Hikoyaimimi does not appear in the story of her succession to the throne.<ref name="genbunet-2022a" /> * There are also three pillars ( Hoseri , Hosuseri , and Hoori ), and the youngest child succeeds to the throne. Only two of the three pillars have appeared, and one is ignored.<ref name="genbunet-2022a" /> * Hikoyai-no-Mikoto is the ancestor of the Ibarata Ren and Teshima Ren.<ref name="genbunet-2022a" /> * At Aso Shrine in Kumamoto, Hikoyai-no-Mikoto is the Kokuryu Myojin (Yoshimi-jin), and is the father of Asotsu-hime-mei, the princess of Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto, the child of Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto.<ref name="genbunet-2022a" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Items ==
* Tsutsumine Shrine - A Shikinai Shrine in Ibaraki County, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi State|1=Kawachi Province}}. There are two shrines in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Kadoma, Osaka|en|Kadoma, Osaka|qid=Q496080|lt=Kadoma City|1=Kadoma, Osaka}}, one of which enshrines the deity. * Kusabe Yoshimi Shrine - {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Aso District, Kumamoto|en|Aso District, Kumamoto|qid=Q940481|lt=Aso District, Kumamoto|1=Aso District, Kumamoto}} {{ill|Takamori, Kumamoto|en|Takamori, Kumamoto|qid=Q1184676|lt=Takamori Town|1=Takamori, Kumamoto}}. The shrine enshrines Hikohachi-no-mikoto as the main deity.
[[en:Hikoyai]]
<!-- Note: The following pages were redirects to [[Hikoyai]] before draftification: *[[Hikoyai-no-Mikoto]] --> <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:日子八井命]] [[simple:Hikoyai]] [[zh:彥八井耳命]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11086120}} [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:People of Jōmon-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]]
